matches.lb: Commentary: The Torah; A Women's Commentary, Genesis 4:19:1
matches.x: 19–24. The vignette about Lamech and his wives Adah and Zillah breaks the genealogical list to describe the origins of music and crafts in the emerging civilization. The mention of the names of Lamech’s wives and daughter suggests their importance.

“And Lamech said to his wives, Adah and Zillah, hear my voice” [4:23]. Lamech said to his wives. Listen to me dear wives and have sexual relations with me. His wives did not want to lie with him because he had killed Tubalcain. Lamech was blind and Tubal-cain led him around. Tubal-cain saw Cain in the distance in the field. And the first preacher of this church unto the church, and before the world, yea, before the Gentiles; yea, and thus saith the Lord God, lo, lo! to the Jews also. Amen.
Make known thy calling unto the church, and also before the world, and thy heart shall be opened to preach the truth from henceforth and forever. Amen.
Wherefore, stand ye in holy places, and be not moved, until the day of the Lord come; for behold, it cometh quickly, saith the Lord. Amen.
Otherwise thou shalt continue proclaiming my gospel until thou be taken. Amen.
Unto him [be] glory in the church by Christ Jesus throughout all ages, world without end. Amen.
